page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processors. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.5 GHz with a single thread per core, eliminating simultaneous multithreading overhead. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity delivers a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core tests placing in the top 25%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with single-threaded gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth remains average across both small and large block read/write operations. Large-scale LLM inference tasks show limitations, particularly in text generation for both small and large models, which register in the bottom 10% to 25% of benchmarks. Conversely, multi-threaded operations like software compilation and database transactions perform in the middle tier.

The ARM64 architecture provides a cost-efficient profile relative to traditional x86 hardware, offering high resource density and dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage reduces the reliance on external block storage for high-throughput local operations. This hardware configuration is suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose application hosting that can leverage ARM-native binaries and local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by 48 dedicated physical cores of the ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of system memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 20 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU capabilities, though single-core execution speeds and large-scale LLM text generation fall into lower percentiles. The ARM64 architecture delivers qualitative cost efficiency through high resource density and dedicated cores. This instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and software compilation workloads that benefit from local high-speed storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 48 Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores, balanced memory allocation, and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores.
Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Delivers strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks.
Features a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and static web serving.
